task_name: add Mistral Instruct v3 chat template

plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here is the complete implementation plan:

---

## Plan: `mistral-instruct-v3` Specialized Handler

**Only one file changes:** `common/chat.cpp`

### Step 1 — Add the handler function (~line 1517, after `gigachat_v3` ends)

`static common_chat_params common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(...)` following the standard handler pattern:

- Renders the prompt with `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl(tmpl, inputs)`
- `format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE`, `supports_thinking = false`
- `preserved_tokens = { "[INST]", "[/INST]", "[TOOL_CALLS]" }`
- For **content-only** responses: `p.literal(generation_prompt) + p.content(p.rest())`
- For **tool calls**: uses the existing `p.standard_json_tools()` helper (declared in `chat-peg-parser.h` lines 108–119) with:
  - `section_start = "[TOOL_CALLS]"`, `array_wrapped = true`, `call_id_key = "id"`
  - This precisely models the Nemo/v0.3 format: `[TOOL_CALLS][{"name":"…","arguments":{…},"id":"…"}]`
- Grammar trigger on word `[TOOL_CALLS]`; lazy when `tool_choice == AUTO`

### Step 2 — Register detection (~line 1690, before `return std::nullopt`)

```cpp
// Mistral Instruct v0.3 / Nemo — [INST]/[/INST] without [SYSTEM_PROMPT]
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[/INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v0.3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

### Why detection is safe

The Ministral 3 check (runs first, requires `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`) catches all three other Mistral-family templates in the repo (`Ministral-3`, `Mistral-Small-3.2`, `Devstral`). Only `mistralai-Mistral-Nemo-Instruct-2407` — which has `[INST]`/`[/INST]` but no `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` — falls through to the new check. No non-Mistral template in the repo uses `[INST]`/`[/INST]`.

details.reasoning: The implementation successfully creates the new handler function, implements template auto-detection with proper [SYSTEM_PROMPT] exclusion check, includes the LOG_DBG call, dispatches correctly from the template detection function, and makes no modifications to existing handlers. However, it has significant gaps: (1) Uses COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE instead of the required COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY, (2) No explicit logic for wrapping user turns with [INST]/[/INST] markers, (3) No system message prepending to first user turn, (4) No </s> appending after assistant turns. These missing message handling features are critical to the template's functionality.
